name: oracle
explanation: The Oracle Database integration requires a dedicated Datadog database user with explicit grants (`username` + `password` are both `required: true`) plus the `service_name` of the target instance. None of these can be derived from the wire — credentials are provisioned out of band. Additionally, Oracle's network protocol requires a proprietary client library: either the bundled native client, the Oracle Instant Client, or a JDBC driver path. This integration ships no Python check inside `integrations-core` (the heavy lifting moved to the Agent core/DBM); only spec, README, and dashboards are present here. Pattern D (credential-required) plus a host-prerequisite, mirroring `ibm_i`'s shape — proprietary client library prerequisite is also a structural blocker.
notes: No `datadog_checks/oracle/` Python package in this repo (Agent-core/DBM integration). Pattern T candidate: proprietary client library prerequisite (Oracle Instant Client / Oracle native client / JDBC driver) shared with `ibm_i`.
